DENIED. PROVIDING UNVERIFIED RESPONSES DOES NOT RENDER THE MOTION MOOT. THE DESIRE TO PRESENT ORAL ARGUMENT IS NOT A NEW FACT THAT WARRANTS RECONSIDERATION. SEE GILBERD V. AC TRANSIT (1995) 32 CAL. APP. 4TH. 1494, 1500. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Omtion For Reconsideration Of The Order Granting Walgreen Co'S Motion To Compel Pltf'S Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jasun C. Molinelli, Esq., Archer Norris, 925-930-6600 for plaintiffs and moving party: Stacie Ivery and Jerome Ivery
Jo Melnyk, Esq., 510-834-4350, for defendant and opposing party: Walgreen Company (improperly sued as Walgreen's)

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFFS STACIE IVERY and JEROME IVERY'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Order Granting Walgreen Co'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions. DENIED. Providing unverified responses does not render the motion moot. The desire to present oral argument is not a new fact that warrants reconsideration. See Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal. App. 4th. 1494, 1500. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Dec-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jo Melnyk, Esq., 510-834-4350, for defendant and moving party: Walgreen Company (improperly sued as Walgreen's)
Jasun C. Molinelli, Esq., Archer Norris, 925-930-6600 for plaintiffs and opposing party: Stacie Ivery and Jerome Ivery

Argued. Untimely filed opposition was not considered. The court adopts its tentative ruling.
